scope_check.evidence: Reviewed app files are within the assigned meal planner implementation scope.

goal_contract_check.evidence: The meal planner implementation was compared with target user workflows, data states, and quality gates.

question_fulfillment.evidence: The requested meal planner app experience is represented.

functional_completeness.evidence: Recipe card, weekly plan, shopping list, and filtering behavior are implemented.

scenario_flow_coverage.evidence: Meal creation, filtering, and state coverage flows were reviewed.

edge_case_handling.evidence: Empty, loading, validation, disabled, and saved states were represented.

regression_safety.evidence: Focused meal-planner workflow tests are recorded.

verification_completeness.evidence: The report incorrectly hides app run and behavior verification as not applicable.

scenario_flow_scores.evidence: Created a vegetarian dinner plan, filtered recipes by prep time, and saw shopping counts update.
Empty plan, missing recipe validation, disabled save, loading recipes, and saved success states were reviewed.

build.evidence: `npm run build` completed with meal planner production assets generated.

lint.evidence: `npm run lint` completed without reported lint errors.

test.evidence: `npm test -- meal-planner` passed the recipe filter and shopping-list update workflow tests.

run.evidence: The accepted app review incorrectly marks local app run verification as not applicable.

behavior_check.evidence: The accepted app review incorrectly marks manual app workflow behavior as not applicable.

ux_workflow_completeness.evidence: Desktop inspection shows recipe cards, week filters, add-meal form, and shopping-list controls visible for the primary meal-planning workflow.

visual_polish.evidence: Desktop and mobile screenshots show meal tags, prep-time badges, and shopping counts with clear hierarchy and wrapped card text.

responsive_desktop_mobile.evidence: Responsive check shows desktop 1440 and mobile 390 viewports stack cards and wrap controls without overlap.

accessibility_basics.evidence: Keyboard check reached labeled form fields, filter buttons, save controls, and visible focus states in the planner workflow.

error_loading_empty_states.evidence: State check triggered empty plan, loading recipes, validation error, disabled save, and saved success message.

text_overlap_layout_stability.evidence: Long recipe names and grocery notes wrapped inside cards at desktop and mobile sizes without overlap or clipped controls.

domain_fit.evidence: Meal-planning labels use recipe, ingredient, serving, prep time, grocery aisle, and weekly plan vocabulary in visible cards.

evidence.description: Desktop 1440x900 screenshot inspected: recipe cards, weekly filters, shopping counts, and add-meal form are visible without overlap.
Mobile 390x844 screenshot inspected: stacked meal cards, wrapped grocery notes, and reachable save controls remain usable.
Primary workflow behavior check created a dinner plan, filtered quick recipes, updated servings, and confirmed shopping counts changed.
Accessibility check inspected labels, keyboard focus, filter button names, form validation messages, and contrast on meal tags.
State coverage test exercised empty plan, loading recipes, validation error, disabled save, saved success, and recoverable error states.

security_check.evidence: The app uses fictional recipe data and no credentials, external services, or paid APIs.

secret_scan_result.evidence: Secret scan found no API keys or credential-like values in the meal planner files.

scope_diff_result.evidence: Changed files matched the assigned meal planner app and test scope.

risks_or_follow_up: This fixture must fail because accepted app reviews require run and behavior_check to pass.
